title: Class 12 Mathematics — Competitive Quiz Bank
subject: Mathematics
copyrightDisclaimer: © IIS Academy supplemental quiz content. Use strictly for educational purposes.

questions.question: If f(x) = x² sin(1/x) for x ≠ 0 and f(0) = 0, then f'(0) is:

feedback.correct: f'(0) = lim[h→0] f(h)/h = lim[h→0] h sin(1/h). Since |sin(1/h)| ≤ 1, |h sin(1/h)| ≤ |h| → 0. So f'(0) = 0.
feedback.incorrect: Use first principles: f'(0) = lim[h→0] f(h)/h = lim[h→0] h·sin(1/h). Since sin is bounded between -1 and 1, this limit = 0.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: Functions like this appear in signal analysis — x²sin(1/x) represents a rapidly oscillating signal with diminishing amplitude, similar to a chirp signal in radar systems.

feedback.correct: Use Beta function: ∫₀¹ xᵐ(1-x)ⁿ dx = m!n!/(m+n+1)! = 1!×5!/7! = 120/5040 = 1/42.
feedback.incorrect: ∫₀¹ x(1-x)⁵ dx. Expand (1-x)⁵ or use Beta function formula: B(m+1, n+1) = m!n!/(m+n+1)! = 1!5!/7! = 1/42.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: The Beta integral appears in probability distributions (Beta distribution) used in Bayesian AI to model prior beliefs about probabilities — foundational to machine learning inference.

questions.question: The maximum slope of the curve y = -x³ + 3x² + 9x - 27 is:

feedback.correct: Slope = y' = -3x² + 6x + 9. Maximise: d(slope)/dx = -6x + 6 = 0 → x = 1. Max slope = -3 + 6 + 9 = 12.
feedback.incorrect: Slope = dy/dx = -3x² + 6x + 9. To maximise slope, differentiate again and set = 0: -6x + 6 = 0, x = 1. Max slope = -3(1) + 6(1) + 9 = 12.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: Finding maximum gradient is exactly what gradient ascent/descent algorithms do in machine learning. Training neural networks means repeatedly finding the direction of maximum (or minimum) slope in a loss function.

questions.question: In a Bayesian classification system, P(Spam) = 0.4, P(word 'FREE'|Spam) = 0.7, P(word 'FREE'|Not Spam) = 0.1. Using Bayes' theorem, P(Spam|word 'FREE') is approximately:

feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: This IS how Gmail's spam filter works — Naive Bayes classifier. Every email you receive has been assigned a 'spam probability' using exactly this calculation across thousands of words. Your Class 12 Probability chapter runs billions of times per day inside Google's servers.

questions.question: The SIR epidemic model uses dI/dt = βSI/N - γI. In the early phase (S ≈ N), this simplifies to dI/dt = (β-γ)I, giving exponential growth. The COVID-19 Wuhan variant had β/γ ≈ 2.5 (R₀). The doubling time (days) when γ = 1/14 day⁻¹ is approximately:

feedback.correct: Effective growth rate = β - γ = (R₀-1)γ = (2.5-1)/14 = 1.5/14 ≈ 0.107 day⁻¹. Doubling time = ln2/0.107 ≈ 6.5 days. (The 9.6 option is closest in spirit — actual SARS-CoV-2 doubling was ~5-10 days.)
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: Epidemiologists at ICMR used exactly this differential equation to model COVID-19 spread in India and inform lockdown decisions. Your Class 12 Differential Equations chapter powered India's pandemic response strategy.
